steps.title: Set up the linear pressure function
steps.content:
Pressure at any depth equals initial pressure plus the pressure increase from descent.

At sea level (depth = 0): 15.20 psi
For each foot descended: pressure increases by 0.43 psi

This gives us the linear function where $d$ is depth in feet:

steps.title: Substitute depth and calculate
steps.content:
Substitute $d = 110$ feet into the function:
$$P = 15.20 + 0.43(110)$$
Calculate the product: $0.43 \times 110 = 47.3$
$$P = 15.20 + 47.3 = 62.50 \text{ psi}$$
That matches Choice A — $62.50$.
